summary: Partial coverage with real gaps: CONSTRAINTS.md carries the three PRD-stated budgets, but the binding ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md has no NFR/Performance-Budgets section, never binds the budgets to owning components, omits the perf/sync-latency benchmark suites, and several implied hot paths (meeting-closeout end-to-end latency, Copilot response, agent concurrency cap) have no budget at all.

findings.finding: The binding architecture draft has no Non-Functional / Performance-Budgets section at all. The three PRD-stated budgets (dashboard sub-2s after cache warmup; KnowledgeWriter->GBrain search visibility <=60s p95; KnowledgeWriter->dashboard read-model visibility <=10s p95) appear only in the CONSTRAINTS.md planning artifact, never in ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md, and are never bound to the components that must meet them (read-model projector in §4/worker, GBrain adapter in §6, dashboard surfaces in §11). For a production-grade build the contract must carry these budgets and assign ownership/measurement points; otherwise tasks-gen has no anchored target to implement against.
findings.evidence: PRD §17 (lines 1004, 1010) + §5.4 (line 209); REQ-NF-002/003 (REQUIREMENTS.md lines 30-31); budgets present in CONSTRAINTS.md §Performance Budgets (lines 54-58) but absent from ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md (section list §1-§18 + Appendix A has no NFR/perf section; grep for latency/p95/60s/10s in the draft returns only the AgentJob cost/runtime 'budgets' field at line 445)
findings.proposed_fix: Carry an explicit answer into final ARCHITECTURE.md (deferral is a valid recorded answer the owner can confirm).

findings.finding: Add a Performance Budgets subsection to ARCHITECTURE.md that restates each PRD budget, names the owning component, and names the p95 measurement point: (1) dashboard normal-view load sub-2s after warm cache, served from read models with NO live LLM call (defines a 'warm cache' precondition and cold-start exemption); (2) KnowledgeWriter-commit -> GBrain-search-visible <=60s p95, measured at the GBrain index/embed pipeline; (3) KnowledgeWriter-commit -> dashboard-read-model-visible <=10s p95, measured at the read-model projector. Each budget links to the System Health exceedance signal (OBS-2) so a breach is observable, not silent.
findings.evidence: PRD §17 Observability (line 1010) requires exceedances surfaced in System Health; §20.1 'Knowledge write' AUTO test (line 1103) fails a write whose derived surfaces exceed the window; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 (line 144) and §11 (lines 298-300) own read models / dashboard but state no budget
findings.proposed_fix: Insert the subsection with the three budgets, owning components, p95 measurement points, and the System Health breach link.

findings.finding: The Eval and Test Harness section omits the performance and sync-latency benchmark suites that REQ-NF-002 and REQ-NF-003 explicitly require. REQ-NF-002's test is 'Performance smoke/benchmark' and REQ-NF-003's is 'Sync-latency benchmark', and §20.1's 'Knowledge write' AUTO test asserts derived-surface visibility within the <=60s/<=10s windows — yet §12's Required-suites list jumps from EVAL-1 accuracy/retrieval benchmarks straight past any latency benchmark. Under the production-grade posture (dim 9 required) the p95 latency budgets are unverifiable as drafted.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md lines 30-31 (test columns 'Performance smoke/benchmark', 'Sync-latency benchmark'); PRD §20.1 line 1103; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §12 Required suites (lines 319-333) lists no performance/latency suite
findings.proposed_fix: Add a 'Performance & sync-latency benchmark' suite to §12 that measures the three budgets at their p95 points and fails on breach (wired to the §20.1 knowledge-write window assertion).

findings.finding: Meeting closeout is named the primary proof-spine hot path, but no end-to-end latency/duration budget exists anywhere in the PRD or artifacts — only the per-job maxRuntimeSeconds cost-cap kill-switch (a budget-overrun guard, not a UX latency target) and the accuracy metric (90%+ routing). The §11.1 ten-step workflow ends 'Dashboard and Telegram summarize the result' with no bound on how soon the user sees the closeout. This is an implied hot path with no budget; deferral is a valid recorded answer, but it must be recorded, not silently absent.
findings.evidence: PRD §11.1 (lines 685-696), §5.3/§9.4 closeout as P0 (line 186), §13.1 maxRuntimeSeconds (line 801); no closeout time budget in PRD §17 or CONSTRAINTS.md §Performance Budgets
findings.proposed_fix: Ask the owner for a target meeting-closeout end-to-end latency (e.g., p95 from Granola event ingress to dashboard/Telegram summary), or record an explicit deferral with rationale.

findings.finding: Interactive hot paths other than the dashboard have no budget: the Copilot agent query/response (§11 Copilot surface), the approval-action round-trip (Mac/Telegram approve -> effect), and event-ingress -> workflow-start latency (Granola/message lands -> workflow begins). The only named interactive budget is the sub-2s dashboard load; Copilot and approvals are user-facing interactions with no latency target. Deferral is acceptable but should be recorded.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §11 (lines 301, 303) lists Copilot and Approval Inbox; PRD §17 Performance (line 1004) budgets only the dashboard load; CONSTRAINTS.md §Performance Budgets names no interactive budget beyond the dashboard
findings.proposed_fix: Ask the owner whether Copilot response, approval round-trip, and ingress->workflow-start get latency targets, or record explicit deferral.

findings.finding: Maximum concurrent agent jobs per local machine is unset, yet it gates whether the <=10s/<=60s p95 budgets hold under contention on a single Mac. RT-7 requires the control plane to 'cap concurrent agent runs against a single-machine resource budget', but no concurrency number or sizing model exists, and the architecture draft does not name the cap mechanism. CONSTRAINTS.md already flags this (plus default per-job maxCostUsd and provider timeout/retry defaults) as open. Throughput cap is load-bearing for the latency NFRs and the COST-1 budget enforcement.
findings.evidence: PRD RT-7 (line 487) 'cap concurrent agent runs against a single-machine resource budget'; CONSTRAINTS.md lines 62-64 ('[open question] Maximum concurrent agent jobs per local machine', default maxCostUsd, provider timeout/retry); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md names no concurrency cap mechanism
findings.proposed_fix: Ask the owner for a concurrency cap (or a resource-budget sizing rule) and default per-job maxCostUsd / provider timeout-retry defaults, or record explicit deferral; then name the cap mechanism in the broker/control-plane section.
